Description: Nuxt automatically generates routes based on the file structure in the pages/ directory
Do: Create pages in the pages/ directory with proper naming
Don't: Manually configure routes in a separate config file
Code Good: // pages/users/[id].vue

Description: NuxtLink provides optimized client-side navigation with prefetching
Do: Use NuxtLink component for internal links
Don't: Use native anchor tags for internal navigation
Code Good: <NuxtLink to="/about">About</NuxtLink>
Code Bad: About

Description: Use [...slug].vue for dynamic nested routes that can match multiple path segments
Do: Create catch-all routes for dynamic content like CMS pages
Don't: Create individual pages for each possible path

Description: Middleware allows you to run code before navigating to a route for auth guards and redirects
Do: Define middleware in middleware/ directory
Don't: Put navigation guards directly in page components

Description: Use definePageMeta to set page-specific configuration like layout and middleware
Do: Use definePageMeta in script setup
Don't: Set meta in component options API
Code Good:
<script setup>
definePageMeta({
  layout: 'admin',
  middleware: 'auth'
})
</script>

Description: Select SSR/SSG/SPA based on content type and SEO requirements
Do: Use SSR for dynamic SEO content and SSG for static content
Don't: Use client-only rendering for SEO-critical pages

Description: Configure different rendering strategies per route using routeRules
Do: Define routeRules for optimal performance per page type
Don't: Apply same rendering mode to all routes

Description: Use Incremental Static Regeneration for content that changes periodically
Do: Set appropriate swr or isr values in routeRules
Don't: Rebuild entire site for minor content changes
Code Bad:
// Triggering full rebuild for
// each product update

Description: Use ClientOnly component for browser-specific functionality
Do: Wrap client-only code in ClientOnly component
Don't: Import browser-only libraries at top level
Code Good:
<ClientOnly>
  <BrowserOnlyChart :data="data" />
  <template #fallback>
    <ChartSkeleton />
  </template>
</ClientOnly>
Code Bad:
<template>
  <BrowserOnlyChart />
</template>
// Causes SSR errors

Description: useFetch is the recommended way to fetch data with SSR support and caching
Do: Use useFetch or useAsyncData for API calls
Don't: Use raw fetch or axios without composables
Code Good: const { data, pending, error } = await useFetch('/api/users')

Guideline: Provide unique keys for data fetching
Description: Ensure each useFetch/useAsyncData call has a unique key for proper caching
Do: Provide explicit unique keys for each data fetch
Don't: Rely on auto-generated keys that may conflict

Description: Always handle pending and error states from data fetching composables
Do: Display loading indicators and error messages
Don't: Assume data is always available
Code Bad: // Crashes when data is null

Description: Use lazy option to defer data fetching until needed
Do: Use lazy: true for below-fold content
Don't: Fetch all data eagerly on page load

Description: Configure cache behavior for optimal performance and freshness
Do: Set getCachedData for custom caching logic
Don't: Disable caching entirely

Description: Use transform option to shape data before storing in state
Do: Transform API responses to match component needs
Don't: Transform data in computed properties repeatedly

Description: Nuxt has specific lifecycle hooks that differ from Vue's standard hooks
Do: Use Nuxt-specific hooks like useNuxtApp for app-level operations
Don't: Mix Nuxt and Vue lifecycle hooks incorrectly

Description: callOnce ensures code runs only once during SSR and hydration
Do: Use callOnce for analytics or one-time initializations
Don't: Run initialization code that executes multiple times

Description: Ensure server and client render identical content to avoid hydration mismatches
Do: Use consistent data between server and client
Don't: Generate different content on server vs client
Code Good:
const timestamp = useState('time', () => Date.now())
// Same value on both sides
Code Bad: // Different on server/client

Description: Register to app:created app:mounted and other app-level hooks for initialization
Do: Use defineNuxtPlugin for app-level setup
Don't: Put global setup code in components

Description: Nuxt server routes provide a built-in backend API layer
Do: Create API endpoints in server/api/ directory
Don't: Create separate backend projects for simple APIs

Description: Name server routes with HTTP method suffix for clarity
Do: Use .get.ts .post.ts etc for method-specific routes
Don't: Handle all methods in single file with conditionals

Description: Always validate incoming request data on the server
Do: Use zod or similar for request validation
Don't: Trust client-side validation alone

Description: Use createError for consistent error responses
Do: Throw createError with proper status codes
Don't: Return plain error objects or strings

Description: Leverage built-in H3 utilities for common operations
Do: Use getQuery getCookie setHeader and other utilities
Don't: Access raw request objects directly

Description: Use server middleware for cross-cutting concerns like logging and auth
Do: Create middleware in server/middleware/ directory
Don't: Duplicate auth checks in every API route

Description: useState provides SSR-friendly reactive state that hydrates properly
Do: Use useState for state that needs to survive SSR
Don't: Use ref for state that needs SSR support
Code Good: const counter = useState('counter', () => 0)
Code Bad:
const counter = ref(0)
// Lost during hydration

Description: Extract reusable state logic into composables directory
Do: Create composables in composables/ for shared logic
Don't: Duplicate state management across components

Description: Pinia integrates seamlessly with Nuxt for complex state management
Do: Use @pinia/nuxt module for stores
Don't: Use Vuex or custom state management

Description: Ensure state is scoped per-request in SSR to avoid leaking between users
Do: Use useState or Pinia stores properly scoped
Don't: Use module-level reactive state

Description: Reset all user-related state when users log out
Do: Use clearNuxtState or manually reset stores
Don't: Leave stale user data in state

Description: useSeoMeta provides type-safe SEO meta tag management
Do: Use useSeoMeta for all SEO-related meta tags
Don't: Manually create meta tags with useHead
Code Good:
useSeoMeta({
  title: 'My Page',
  ogTitle: 'My Page',
  description: 'Page description',
  ogDescription: 'Page description',
  ogImage: 'https://example.com/image.png'
})

Description: Update meta tags based on page content for better SEO
Do: Compute meta tags from fetched data
Don't: Use static meta tags for dynamic content

Description: Set up proper robots.txt and sitemap for search engines
Do: Use @nuxtjs/sitemap and robots modules
Don't: Manually create robots.txt and sitemap
Code Bad:
// Manual robots.txt in public/
// No dynamic sitemap

Description: Implement JSON-LD structured data for rich search results
Do: Use useHead or useSchemaOrg for structured data
Don't: Omit structured data for content pages
Code Bad:
// No structured data
// Missing rich snippet opportunities

Description: Set canonical URLs to avoid duplicate content issues
Do: Define canonical URLs for all pages
Don't: Allow duplicate URLs without canonicals
Code Bad:
// No canonical URL
// /page and /page/ seen as duplicates

Description: Separate global inline and named middleware appropriately
Do: Create named middleware for reusable logic
Don't: Put all middleware logic inline in pages

Description: Properly handle async operations and redirects in middleware
Do: Return navigateTo or abortNavigation for redirects
Don't: Perform side effects without proper returns

Description: Control middleware execution order with numbered prefixes
Do: Use numbered prefixes for execution order
Don't: Rely on alphabetical order accidentally

Guideline: Use route rules for simple cases
Description: For simple redirects use routeRules instead of middleware
Do: Configure redirects in nuxt.config.ts routeRules
Don't: Create middleware for simple redirects

Description: Implement error.vue for handling application errors gracefully
Do: Create error.vue in root with proper error display
Don't: Let default error page show to users
Code Good: // error.vue
Code Bad:
// No error.vue
// Users see ugly default error

Description: showError triggers the error page for unrecoverable errors
Do: Use showError for page-level fatal errors
Don't: Throw plain errors without proper handling

Description: Use NuxtErrorBoundary for isolated error handling
Do: Wrap risky components in NuxtErrorBoundary
Don't: Let component errors crash the page
Code Bad:
<template>
  <RiskyComponent />
  <!-- Crashes entire page on error -->
</template>

Description: Send errors to external monitoring services like Sentry
Do: Integrate error tracking in plugins
Don't: Only log errors to console

Description: Control link prefetching for optimal performance
Do: Disable prefetch for low-priority links
Don't: Prefetch all links by default
Code Good:
<NuxtLink to="/settings" :prefetch="false">
  Settings
</NuxtLink>
<NuxtLink to="/dashboard" prefetch>
  Dashboard (important)
</NuxtLink>
Code Bad:
<NuxtLink to="/rarely-visited">
  <!-- Prefetches unnecessarily -->
</NuxtLink>

Guideline: Use external prop for outside links
Description: Properly handle external links with NuxtLink
Do: Set external prop for links outside your app
Don't: Use NuxtLink for all external URLs without config
Code Good:
<NuxtLink to="https://external.com" external>
  External Site
</NuxtLink>
Code Bad:
<NuxtLink to="https://external.com">
  External <!-- May cause issues -->
</NuxtLink>

Description: Style active navigation links properly
Do: Use exact or custom active classes
Don't: Ignore active state styling
Code Good:
<NuxtLink
  to="/about"
  active-class="text-primary"
  exact-active-class="font-bold"
>
  About
</NuxtLink>
Code Bad:
<NuxtLink to="/about">
  About <!-- No active indication -->
</NuxtLink>

Description: Nuxt auto-imports composables utils and Vue APIs
Do: Use auto-imported functions directly
Don't: Manually import auto-imported items
Code Good:
<script setup>
const route = useRoute()
const { data } = await useFetch('/api/data')
const count = ref(0)
</script>
Code Bad:
<script setup>
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { useRoute } from 'vue-router'
import { useFetch } from '#app'
// Unnecessary imports
</script>

Description: Add custom directories or imports to auto-import configuration
Do: Configure imports in nuxt.config.ts
Don't: Import utilities manually everywhere
Code Bad:
// Importing manually in every file
import { formatDate } from '~/utils/date'

Description: Enable explicit imports in large codebases for better IDE support
Do: Use #imports for explicit imports when needed
Don't: Disable auto-imports entirely
Code Good:
<script setup>
import { useFetch, useRoute } from '#imports'
// Explicit for clarity
</script>

Description: Use plugins for app-wide initialization and third-party integrations
Do: Register plugins in plugins/ directory
Don't: Initialize global code in components

Description: Use provide/inject pattern in plugins for dependency injection
Do: Provide services through plugins
Don't: Create singleton instances in utils

Description: Specify plugin dependencies and ordering
Do: Use dependsOn for plugin dependencies
Don't: Rely on file naming for critical order

Guideline: Use runtime config for environment variables
Description: runtimeConfig provides type-safe access to environment variables
Do: Define variables in runtimeConfig
Don't: Access process.env directly in components

Description: Keep sensitive values in private runtimeConfig
Do: Put client-safe values in runtimeConfig.public only
Don't: Expose secrets in public config

Description: Add TypeScript types for runtime config
Do: Extend RuntimeConfig interface for type safety
Don't: Use untyped config access
Code Bad:
const config = useRuntimeConfig()
config.unknownKey // No type error

Description: Use dynamic imports for heavy components
Do: Lazy load heavy components with defineAsyncComponent
Don't: Import all components statically
Code Bad:
import HeavyChart from '~/components/HeavyChart.vue'
// Loaded even if not shown

Description: Extract and cache payloads for static routes
Do: Enable payloadExtraction for prerendered routes
Don't: Inline all data in HTML
Code Bad:
// All data inlined
// Larger HTML files

Description: Isolate interactive components to reduce JS bundle
Do: Use NuxtIsland for isolated interactivity
Don't: Ship all JS for every component
Code Bad:
// All components ship JS
// Even mostly static pages
